name: Bajaj Auto Ltd

verification.method: Cell-by-cell from Bajaj Auto Limited standalone audited financial statements across the FY16-FY25 annual reports (9th AR FY2015-16 through 18th AR FY2024-25), sourced from BSE corporate filings (scripcode 532977). The FY16-FY22 back-catalogue was pulled by the BSE scraper (scripts/scrape-bse-company.mjs), text-extracted (scripts/extract-bse-ars.mjs) into src/data/source-text/BAJAJ_<year>.txt, then parsed line-by-line with quoted source references (data/raw-bajaj-backfill.json) and assembled by scripts/backfill-from-raw.mjs. Derived items (EBITDA, EBIT, Net Debt, Working Capital, FCF, margins, growth, D/E, ROE, ROCE) computed by formula from disclosed line items per the same methodology used for FY23-FY25 and for TVS. No estimates: every populated cell is a disclosed figure or a formula over disclosed figures, and all accounting cross-checks in scripts/audit-data.mjs pass.
verification.upgradePath: Coverage complete FY16-FY25 for P&L, Balance Sheet and Cash Flow. Remaining nulls are not-applicable (Bajaj makes no scooters/mopeds), first-year-undefined (FY16 YoY growth), or paid-source-only items (model-wise volume split, CC-slab share, capacity utilisation) listed in `na` / dataStatus.

sources.primary: Bajaj Auto Limited Annual Reports FY2015-16 through FY2024-25 (9th-18th Annual Reports), standalone audited financial statements, sourced from BSE (scripcode 532977). Recent-year auditors: S R B C & CO LLP (ICAI Firm Reg. 324982E/E300003).
sources.notes: Standalone basis (not Consolidated). Consolidated KTM / PBAG / BACL / BATL financials excluded. EBITDA derived = PBT + Finance costs + Depreciation - Other income (operating EBITDA excl. other income), one convention across FY16-FY25. EBIT = EBITDA - Depreciation. Net Debt = Total borrowings - (Cash + Other bank balances + Current investments + Non-current investments, excl. investments in subsidiaries). Working Capital = Receivables + Inventory - Trade Payables. Capex = Purchase of PP&E + Expenditure on technical know-how/intangibles. ROCE = EBIT / (Net worth + Total debt). Basis: FY16 Indian GAAP, FY17+ Ind AS; FY16-FY18 revenue is stated NET of excise duty for comparability with post-GST years (FY19+). FY18 (-32 Cr charge), FY19 (+342 Cr NCCD reversal) and FY22 (+315 Cr) carry exceptional items that sit within reported PBT and the derived EBITDA for those years.

Note: FY19 PBT includes a net exceptional gain of ₹342.00 Cr, which also sits within the derived EBITDA for the year.

perFY.FY23: Bajaj Auto 17th AR (FY24) · Standalone comparatives · Balance Sheet pp. 131-132, P&L pp. 133-134, Cash Flow pp. 137-139, MD&A Table 6 p. 28
perFY.FY24: Bajaj Auto 17th AR (FY24) · Standalone audited statements · pp. 131-139 + MD&A Table 6 p. 28 + Directors' Report Sales table p. 35; cross-checked against Bajaj Auto 18th AR (FY25) comparatives pp. 141-149 + MD&A Table 6 p. 30
perFY.FY25: Bajaj Auto 18th AR (FY25) · Standalone audited statements · Balance Sheet pp. 141-142, P&L pp. 143-144, Cash Flow pp. 147-149, MD&A Table 6 p. 30, Directors' Report Sales table p. 35, Export Table 4 p. 16, 3W Table 2 p. 14, Chetak Table 3 p. 15

threeWheelerSplitFy25.source: Bajaj Auto 18th AR (FY25) Table 2 'Domestic Sale of Three-Wheelers' p. 14. Note: domestic only; export 3W = 189,221 sits separately and is mostly ICE. Total 3W = 668,657 reconciles: domestic ICE 421,119 + domestic EV 58,197 + exports 189,221 = 668,537 (minor reconciliation gap due to reporting boundary).

notes.motorcycles: Includes 2W brands: Pulsar, CT, Platina, Discover, Avenger, Dominar, Freedom (CNG bike, world's first), KTM, Husqvarna, Triumph (CKD), and Chetak EV scooter. AR Sales-in-Numbers table reports a single 'Two-wheelers' line — Bajaj Chetak EV is included in that total, so 'motorcycles' here is the AR's 'Two-wheelers' aggregate (not 100% road-motorcycles).
notes.scooters: Bajaj exited the conventional scooter business in 2009. The Chetak EV (re-launched 2020) is currently reported by management within the 'Two-wheelers' aggregate, not broken out. Treated as null here for the residual scooter line; EV volume captured separately in evByFy.
notes.mopeds: Bajaj does not manufacture mopeds. All FYs null.
notes.threeWheelers: Bajaj is India's #1 three-wheeler manufacturer (FY25 domestic ICE share ~75.7%, EV share ~35.8%). 'Commercial vehicles' line in the AR Sales-in-Numbers table = three-wheelers (passenger + goods) + quadricycle.
notes.ev: Chetak electric scooter, domestic only. Source: AR MD&A Table 3 'Domestic Sale of Chetak EV'. Export EV figures not separately disclosed.
notes.exports: Total export volume (2W + CV). FY22 export peak of 2.506m disclosed in FY25 AR Table 4 retrospectively. Source: FY25 AR p. 16 Table 4 'Export Volume and Value for Bajaj Auto'.

statusLegend.available: Disclosed in Bajaj Auto standalone audited statements / Directors' Report / MD&A; explicit value lifted.
statusLegend.derived: Computed from disclosed line items (e.g. Domestic = Total - Exports; ICE = Total - EV). Each derived cell carries the underlying disclosure source.
statusLegend.pending_pdf_parse: Value exists in an annual report not yet text-extracted. The FY16–FY22 back-catalogue has now been extracted from BSE filings, so no cell currently carries this status.
statusLegend.unavailable: Not disclosed by Bajaj Auto in any public document.
statusLegend.unavailable_pre_ev: Bajaj Chetak EV launched October 2019 (FY20). Pre-FY20 EV mix is exactly 0 by virtue of the product not existing.
statusLegend.unavailable_minimal_ev: EV volumes existed but were immaterial and not broken out separately in audited statements.
statusLegend.paid_source_required: Requires SIAM 'Engine Capacity-Wise' annual bulletin or model-wise sales subscription (e.g. JATO Dynamics). Cannot be derived from Bajaj Auto standalone audited statements alone.

na: Gross Margin (cost-of-materials disclosed but excludes traded-goods purchase + COGS not separately reconciled)
Model-wise volume split (Pulsar / CT / Platina / Avenger / Dominar / Freedom)

profile.source: Bajaj Auto Limited 18th Annual Report 2024-25 — Corporate Information, Directors' Report, BRSR section, MD&A.

kmp.managingDirectorNote: Re-appointed as MD for a 5-year term per 18th AR Section III.
kmp.cooNote: Bajaj Auto does not list a separate COO; operations split across two Executive Directors (Shrivastava: manufacturing; Sharma: business).

employees.permanentNote: Directors' Report Section (E): 'Permanent employees as on 31 March 2025: 5598'. Excludes trainees, probationers and contract employees.
employees.permanentFy24Note: Bajaj Auto 17th AR (FY24) Directors' Report Section E: 'Permanent employees as on 31 March 2024: 6,192'.

dealers.totalNote: FY25 AR does not aggregate a single 'total dealerships' figure for Bajaj Auto India. Disclosed network components include: 1,217 TPM-practicing dealerships (India); >4,000 Chetak EV touchpoints; 850+ GoGo (electric 3W) dealerships; 136 Triumph premium showrooms across 100 cities.
dealers.evTouchpointsNote: FY25 AR MD&A p. 15 — 'Rapid expansion of network to >4,000 touchpoints propelled the business to leadership.'
dealers.gogoDealershipsNote: FY25 AR MD&A p. 15 — 'GoGo brand with features loaded products and a network of 850+ dealerships'.
dealers.triumphShowroomsNote: FY25 AR MD&A p. 14 — '136 premium showrooms across 100 cities'.

creditRating.longTermScope: Long-term bank loan facilities; Stable outlook
creditRating.shortTermScope: Short-term bank loan facilities; Stable outlook
creditRating.source: FY25 AR Directors' Report 'Credit Rating' section — 'CRISIL Ratings has affirmed its long-term rating of CRISIL AAA and short-term rating of CRISIL A1+ with Stable outlook ... India Ratings and Research (Ind-Ra) has also rated its long-term rating of IND AAA and short-term rating of IND A1+ with Stable outlook for the bank loan facilities of the Company.'

facilitiesInternational: Manaus, Brazil (Bajaj Do Brasil — 20,000 units/yr motorcycle plant, operational H2 2024)

installedCapacityByPlant.source: Bajaj Auto 18th AR (FY25) pp. 1092-1097 manufacturing capacity table. Waluj: Boxer, CT, Platina, Discover, Freedom, Pulsar; CV at Waluj: Passenger / Goods Carriers, Quadricycle. Chakan 1: Pulsar, Avenger, Dominar. Chakan 2: KTM, Husqvarna, Triumph. Chakan 1/Akurdi: Chetak, Yulu. Pantnagar: CT, Platina, Freedom, Pulsar.

auditors.statutory: S R B C & CO LLP
auditors.signingPartner: Paul Alvares (Membership 105754) — FY25 AR; Arvind Sethi (Membership 089802) — FY24 AR
auditors.source: Statutory auditor signature blocks on Balance Sheet, P&L, and Cash Flow in both 17th AR and 18th AR.

subsidiaries.BAIHBV: Bajaj Auto International Holdings BV (Netherlands) — 100% owned; holds 49.9% in Pierer Bajaj AG (KTM / Husqvarna / GASGAS exposure).
subsidiaries.BACL: Bajaj Auto Credit Ltd. — 100% captive financing NBFC. RBI CoR 29-Aug-2023, operations from 01-Jan-2024. FY25: AUM > Rs.9,300 cr, disbursements > Rs.10,000 cr (751k loans), PBT Rs.78 cr, PAT Rs.58 cr.
subsidiaries.BATL: Bajaj Auto Technology Ltd (formerly Chetak Technology Ltd) — 100% subsidiary, EV powertrain & components, Rs.470 cr paid-up.
subsidiaries.BdoBrasil: Bajaj Do Brasil Comercio De Motocicletas Ltda — 100% subsidiary; CY24 sold >13,000 units (3x of CY23); Manaus plant operational H2 2024.
subsidiaries.BAThailand: Bajaj Auto (Thailand) Ltd — 100% R&D / design centre.
subsidiaries.BASpain: Bajaj Auto Spain S.L.U. — 100% engineering design centre, Barcelona.
